name: Reverse Fleckerl
following_figure_names: Contra Check, Reverse Turn, Reverse Fleckerl
notes: The amount of turn: can do 1 full turn up to 2 full turns (as shown above). For both Man and Lady, there is a strong rotation, so there must be a foot swivel on the ball of the flat foot on each step. Fleckerls are done on the spot, it doesn't move around, focus on rotation. The power for the rotation does not come from upper body. Speed comes from feet and legs. When the steps are repeated, the Man will dance step 1 as lady's step 4 (draw LF back to RF, L heel closes to inside edge of RF). Note: Commencing facing LOD (any other alignment is possible). This figure can turn 1 full turn or up to 2 turns, up to dancers.

steps.action: LF fwd, toe turned out (small step)
RF to side and slightly back, with 1/2 weight
LF crosses in front of RF
RF to side and slightly back to inside edge of Man's LF
LF crosses behind RF, 1/2 weight (a type of twist turn)
